# Serving Engines and Runtime Map

Serving engines are where model mechanics meet scheduler policy.

## Chapter Job

Map the runtime ecosystem and explain why the book uses a framework-first path.

## Runtime Families

- vLLM.
- SGLang.
- TensorRT-LLM.
- Triton Inference Server.
- Text Generation Inference.
- llama.cpp and local runtimes.
- Managed provider runtimes.

## Comparison Axes

- Scheduler design.
- KV cache management.
- Continuous batching.
- Prefix caching.
- Structured output and tool support.
- Quantization support.
- Tensor and pipeline parallel support.
- Observability hooks.
- Deployment maturity.

## Book Default

The primary implementation path is vLLM because it is practical, visible, widely
used, and exposes the right concepts for learning.
